pi_items.source: Consistent across setup_task.sh (line 8) and export_result.sh (line 7). Path created synthetically during task setup.
Confirmed in export_result.sh (line 8) and setup_task.sh (lines 95-142). File created synthetically with Kotlin DSL syntax using plugins and android configuration block.
Confirmed in task.json description (line 5) and export_result.sh (line 43). This is the standard JaCoCo HTML report output path following Gradle plugin conventions.

Task description (line 5, section 3d) explicitly specifies all 7 exclusion patterns. Metadata field contains only 3 of these 7 patterns, omitting: **/R$*.class, **/*Test*.*, **/databinding/**, **/android/**. Verified against official Gradle JaCoCo plugin documentation and Android development best practices.

Confirmed in task.json description (line 5, section 3) stating 'Register a custom Gradle task named 'jacocoTestReport''. This is the standard name generated by the Gradle JaCoCo plugin when java plugin is applied. Verified against official Gradle JaCoCo plugin documentation.

Setup_task.sh line 62 specifies distributionUrl=https://services.gradle.org/distributions/gradle-8.5-bin.zip. Gradle 8.5 is a real release (https://docs.gradle.org/8.5/release-notes.html) supporting Java 21 and featuring Kotlin DSL improvements.

Setup_task.sh line 20 specifies id('com.android.application') version '8.2.2'. AGP 8.2.2 is a real release from November 2023 (Android Gradle Plugin 8.2.0 release notes confirm 8.2.x series). Supports compileSdk 34 as used in the task.
Setup_task.sh line 21 specifies id('org.jetbrains.kotlin.android') version '1.9.22'. This is a real Kotlin version from early 2024 compatible with Android Gradle Plugin 8.2.2.

Task description (line 5, section 2) specifies 'Configure JaCoCo with toolVersion set to "0.8.12" (or a compatible 0.8.x version)'. JaCoCo 0.8.12 is a real release from April 2, 2024 (https://www.jacoco.org/jacoco/trunk/doc/changes.html and Maven Central). It adds support for Java 21 and improves ASM version to 9.7.

Setup_task.sh lines 125-126 specify sourceCompatibility = JavaVersion.VERSION_17 and targetCompatibility = JavaVersion.VERSION_17. Environment env.json (line 88) confirms JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64. Java 17 is compatible with all specified tools.
Setup_task.sh line 108 specifies targetSdk = 34. This is compatible with Android Gradle Plugin 8.2.2 which supports API level 34.
Setup_task.sh line 103 specifies compileSdk = 34. This is the target API level for the project.

privileged_info_summary: The add_jacoco_coverage task configures JaCoCo (Java Code Coverage) reporting in an Android project. Key verified facts: The project uses real, publicly available tools including Gradle 8.5, Android Gradle Plugin 8.2.2, Kotlin 1.9.22, and JaCoCo 0.8.12 (released April 2, 2024). The task targets Java 17 and Android API level 34. The project directory is created synthetically at /home/ga/AndroidStudioProjects/WeatherApp with a synthetic WeatherApp project structure. The build configuration uses Kotlin DSL (build.gradle.kts) and the expected JaCoCo report path is app/build/reports/jacoco/jacocoTestReport/html/index.html. CRITICAL DISCREPANCY: The metadata field 'required_exclusions' claims only 3 exclusion patterns ([**/R.class, **/BuildConfig.*, **/Manifest*.*]), but the task description explicitly requires 7 patterns including **/R$*.class, **/*Test*.*, **/databinding/**, and **/android/**. This is a factual contradiction that affects task verification.
